WPFTweaksActivity.Description: Erases recent docs, clipboard, and run history.

WPFTweaksHiber.Description: Hibernation is really meant for laptops as it saves what's in memory before turning the PC off. It really should never be used.

WPFTweaksWidget.Description: Removes the annoying widgets in the bottom left of the Taskbar.
InvokeScript:
# Sometimes if you dont stop the Widgets process the removal may fail

      Get-Process *Widget* | Stop-Process
      Get-AppxPackage Microsoft.WidgetsPlatformRuntime -AllUsers | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers
      Get-AppxPackage MicrosoftWindows.Client.WebExperience -AllUsers | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers

      Invoke-WinUtilExplorerUpdate -action "restart"
      Write-Host "Removed widgets"
UndoScript:
Write-Host "Restoring widgets AppxPackages"

      Add-AppxPackage -Register "C:\Program Files\WindowsApps\Microsoft.WidgetsPlatformRuntime*\AppxManifest.xml" -DisableDevelopmentMode
      Add-AppxPackage -Register "C:\Program Files\WindowsApps\MicrosoftWindows.Client.WebExperience*\AppxManifest.xml" -DisableDevelopmentMode

      Invoke-WinUtilExplorerUpdate -action "restart"

WPFTweaksRevertStartMenu.Description: Bring back the old Start Menu layout from before the gradual rollout of the new one in 25H2.
InvokeScript:
Invoke-WebRequest https://github.com/thebookisclosed/ViVe/releases/download/v0.3.4/ViVeTool-v0.3.4-IntelAmd.zip -OutFile ViVeTool.zip

      Expand-Archive ViVeTool.zip
      Remove-Item ViVeTool.zip

      Start-Process 'ViVeTool\ViVeTool.exe' -ArgumentList '/disable /id:47205210' -Wait -NoNewWindow

      Remove-Item ViVeTool -Recurse

      Write-Host 'Old start menu reverted. Please restart your computer to take effect.'
UndoScript:
Invoke-WebRequest https://github.com/thebookisclosed/ViVe/releases/download/v0.3.4/ViVeTool-v0.3.4-IntelAmd.zip -OutFile ViVeTool.zip

      Expand-Archive ViVeTool.zip
      Remove-Item ViVeTool.zip

      Start-Process 'ViVeTool\ViVeTool.exe' -ArgumentList '/enable /id:47205210' -Wait -NoNewWindow

      Remove-Item ViVeTool -Recurse

      Write-Host 'New start menu reverted. Please restart your computer to take effect.'

WPFTweaksDisableStoreSearch.Content: Microsoft Store Recommended Search Results - Disable
WPFTweaksDisableStoreSearch.Description: Will not display recommended Microsoft Store apps when searching for apps in the Start menu.

WPFTweaksServices.Description: Sets some services to Manual startup and adjusts the SvcHostSplitThresholdInKB registry value to better match system memory, which can significantly reduce the number of svchost.exe processes.

WPFTweaksBraveDebloat.Description: Disables various annoyances like Brave Rewards, Leo AI, Crypto Wallet and VPN.

WPFTweaksDisableWarningForUnsignedRdp.Description: Disables warnings shown when launching unsigned RDP files introduced with the latest Windows 10 and 11 updates.

WPFTweaksEdgeDebloat.Description: Disables various telemetry options, popups, and other annoyances in Edge.

WPFTweaksConsumerFeatures.Description: Windows will not automatically install any games, third-party apps, or application links from the Windows Store for the signed-in user. Some default Apps will be inaccessible (eg. Phone Link).

InvokeScript:
# Disable Defender Auto Sample Submission
      Set-MpPreference -SubmitSamplesConsent 2

      # Disable (Connected User Experiences and Telemetry) Service
      Set-Service -Name diagtrack -StartupType Disabled

      # Disable (Windows Error Reporting Manager) Service
      Set-Service -Name wermgr -StartupType Disabled

      Remove-ItemProperty -Path "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules" -Name PeriodInNanoSeconds
UndoScript:
# Enable Defender Auto Sample Submission
      Set-MpPreference -SubmitSamplesConsent 1

      # Enable (Connected User Experiences and Telemetry) Service
      Set-Service -Name diagtrack -StartupType Automatic

      # Enable (Windows Error Reporting Manager) Service
      Set-Service -Name wermgr -StartupType Automatic

WPFTweaksRemoveEdge.Description: Unblocks Microsoft Edge uninstaller restrictions then uses that uninstaller to remove Microsoft Edge.
UndoScript:
Write-Host 'Installing Microsoft Edge...'
      winget install Microsoft.Edge --source winget

WPFTweaksUTC.Content: Date & Time - Set Time to UTC
WPFTweaksUTC.Description: Essential for computers that are dual booting. Fixes the time sync with Linux systems.

WPFTweaksRemoveOneDrive.Description: Denies permission to remove OneDrive user files, then uses its own uninstaller to remove it and restores the original permission afterward.
InvokeScript:
# Deny permission to remove OneDrive folder
      icacls $Env:OneDrive /deny "Administrators:(D,DC)"

      Write-Host "Uninstalling OneDrive..."
      Start-Process 'C:\Windows\System32\OneDriveSetup.exe' -ArgumentList '/uninstall' -Wait

      # Some of OneDrive files use explorer, and OneDrive uses FileCoAuth
      Write-Host "Removing leftover OneDrive Files..."
      Stop-Process -Name FileCoAuth,Explorer
      Remove-Item "$Env:LocalAppData\Microsoft\OneDrive" -Recurse -Force
      Remove-Item "C:\ProgramData\Microsoft OneDrive" -Recurse -Force

      # Grant back permission to access OneDrive folder
      icacls $Env:OneDrive /grant "Administrators:(D,DC)"

      # Disable OneSyncSvc
      Set-Service -Name OneSyncSvc -StartupType Disabled
UndoScript:
Write-Host "Installing OneDrive"
      winget install Microsoft.Onedrive --source winget

      # Enabled OneSyncSvc
      Set-Service -Name OneSyncSvc -StartupType Automatic

WPFTweaksRemoveHome.Content: File Explorer Home and Gallery - Disable
WPFTweaksRemoveHome.Description: Removes the Home and Gallery from Explorer and sets This PC as default.

WPFTweaksDisplay.Content: Visual Effects - Set to Best Performance
WPFTweaksDisplay.Description: Sets the system preferences to performance. You can do this manually with sysdm.cpl as well.

WPFTweaksXboxRemoval.Description: Removes Xbox services, the Xbox app, Game Bar, and related authentication components.

WPFTweaksDeBloat.Description: This will remove a bunch of Windows pre-installed applications which most people dont want on their system.
InvokeScript:
$TeamsPath = "$Env:LocalAppData\Microsoft\Teams\Update.exe"

      if (Test-Path $TeamsPath) {
        Write-Host "Uninstalling Teams"
        Start-Process $TeamsPath -ArgumentList -uninstall -wait

        Write-Host "Deleting Teams directory"
        Remove-Item $TeamsPath -Recurse -Force
      }

WPFTweaksRestorePoint.Description: Creates a restore point at runtime in case a revert is needed from WinUtil modifications.

WPFTweaksEndTaskOnTaskbar.Content: End Task With Right Click - Enable
WPFTweaksEndTaskOnTaskbar.Description: Enables option to end task when right clicking a program in the taskbar.

WPFTweaksPowershell7Tele.Description: Creates an Environment Variable called 'POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT' with a value of '1' which will tell PowerShell 7 to not send Telemetry Data.

WPFTweaksStorage.Description: Storage Sense deletes temp files automatically.

WPFTweaksWindowsAI.Description: Removes or disables all ai features and packages
InvokeScript:
$Appx = (Get-AppxPackage MicrosoftWindows.Client.CoreAI).PackageFullName
      $Sid = (Get-LocalUser $Env:UserName).Sid.Value

      New-Item "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Appx\AppxAllUserStore\EndOfLife\$Sid\$Appx" -Force

      Get-AppxPackage -AllUsers *Copilot* | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers
      Get-AppxPackage -AllUsers Microsoft.MicrosoftOfficeHub | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers
      Remove-AppxPackage $Appx

      Set-Service -Name WSAIFabricSvc -StartupType Disabled
      Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -FeatureName Recall -Online

      Write-Host "Windows AI Disabled"

WPFTweaksWPBT.Description: If enabled, WPBT allows your computer vendor to execute programs at boot time, such as anti-theft software, software drivers, as well as force install software without user consent. Poses potential security risk.

WPFTweaksRazerBlock.Description: Blocks ALL Razer Software installations. The hardware works fine without any software.

WPFTweaksBlockAdobeNet.Description: Reduces user interruptions by selectively blocking connections to Adobe's activation and telemetry servers. Credit: Ruddernation-Designs
InvokeScript:
$hostsUrl = "https://github.com/Ruddernation-Designs/Adobe-URL-Block-List/raw/refs/heads/master/hosts"
      $hosts = "$Env:SystemRoot\System32\drivers\etc\hosts"

      Move-Item $hosts "$hosts.bak"
      Invoke-WebRequest $hostsUrl -OutFile $hosts
      ipconfig /flushdns

      Write-Host "Added Adobe url block list from host file"
UndoScript:
$hosts = "$Env:SystemRoot\System32\drivers\etc\hosts"

      Remove-Item $hosts
      Move-Item "$hosts.bak" $hosts
      ipconfig /flushdns

      Write-Host "Removed Adobe url block list from host file"

WPFTweaksRightClickMenu.Description: Restores the classic context menu when right-clicking in File Explorer, replacing the simplified Windows 11 version.
UndoScript:
Remove-Item -Path "HKCU:\Software\Classes\CLSID\{86ca1aa0-34aa-4e8b-a509-50c905bae2a2}" -Recurse -Confirm:$false -Force
      # Restarting Explorer in the Undo Script might not be necessary, as the Registry change without restarting Explorer does work, but just to make sure.
      Write-Host Restarting explorer.exe ...
      Stop-Process -Name "explorer" -Force

WPFTweaksDiskCleanup.Description: Runs Disk Cleanup on Drive C: and removes old Windows Updates.

WPFTweaksIPv46.Description: Setting the IPv4 preference can have latency and security benefits on private networks where IPv6 is not configured.

WPFTweaksTeredo.Description: Teredo network tunneling is an IPv6 feature that can cause additional latency, but may cause problems with some games.
InvokeScript: netsh interface teredo set state disabled
UndoScript: netsh interface teredo set state default

WPFTweaksDisableBGapps.Description: Disables all Microsoft Store apps from running in the background, which has to be done individually since Windows 11.

WPFTweaksDisableFSO.Description: Disables FSO in all applications. NOTE: This will disable Color Management in Exclusive Fullscreen.

WPFToggleDetailedBSoD.Description: If enabled, you will see a detailed Blue Screen of Death (BSOD) with more information.

WPFToggleScrollbars.Description: If enabled, scrollbars will always be visible. If disabled, Windows will automatically hide scrollbars when not in use.

WPFToggleHideSettingsHome.Description: Enable or disable the Home Page in the Windows Settings app.

WPFToggleShowExt.Description: If enabled, File extensions (e.g., .txt, .jpg) are visible.

WPFToggleHiddenFiles.Description: If enabled, Hidden Files will be shown.

WPFToggleLongPaths.Description: Enables support for file paths longer than 260 characters.

WPFToggleTaskbarAlignment.Description: [Windows 11] If enabled, the Taskbar Items will be shown on the Center, otherwise the Taskbar Items will be shown on the Left.

WPFToggleTaskbarSearch.Description: If enabled, Search Button will be on the Taskbar.

WPFToggleTaskView.Description: If enabled, Task View Button in Taskbar will be shown.

WPFToggleBatteryPercentage.Description: If enabled, Shows numeric battery percentage next to the battery icon in the system tray.

WPFToggleStartMenuRecommendations.Description: If disabled, then you will not see recommendations in the Start Menu. WARNING: This will also disable Windows Spotlight on your Lock Screen as a side effect.

WPFToggleBingSearch.Description: If enabled, Bing web search results will be included in your Start Menu search.

WPFToggleLoginBlur.Description: If disabled, the acrylic blur effect will be removed on the Windows 10/11 login screen background.

WPFToggleVerboseLogon.Description: Show detailed messages during the login process for troubleshooting and diagnostics.

WPFToggleStandbyFix.Description: Enable or disable network connectivity during S0 Sleep.

WPFToggleS3Sleep.Description: Toggles between Modern Standby and S3 Sleep.

WPFToggleGameMode.Description: If enabled, Windows prioritizes gaming performance by allocating system resources. Disable for audio/video production to prevent interference.

WPFToggleHAGS.Description: If enabled, GPU manages its own VRAM, reducing latency and improving frame consistency. Recommended for gaming. Requires reboot.

WPFToggleWindowedGames.Description: If enabled, improves performance for DX10/11 games in windowed/borderless mode. Disable if you experience stuttering or flickering.

WPFToggleMultiplaneOverlay.Description: Enable or disable the Multiplane Overlay, which can sometimes cause issues with graphics cards.

WPFToggleStickyKeys.Description: If enabled, Sticky Keys is activated. Sticky keys is an accessibility feature of some graphical user interfaces which assists users who have physical disabilities or help users reduce repetitive strain injury.

WPFToggleMouseAcceleration.Description: If enabled, the Cursor movement is affected by the speed of your physical mouse movements.

WPFToggleNumLock.Description: Toggle the Num Lock key state when your computer starts.

WPFToggleNewOutlook.Description: If disabled, it removes the new Outlook toggle, disables the new Outlook migration, and ensures the classic Outlook application is used.

WPFToggleDisableCrossDeviceResume.Description: This tweak controls the Resume function in Windows 11 24H2 and later, which allows you to resume an activity from a mobile device and vice-versa.

WPFToggleDriverUpdate.Content: Driver Updates via Windows Update - Disable
WPFToggleDriverUpdate.Description: If enabled, Windows Update will not install driver updates automatically. Prevents buggy GPU drivers from replacing working ones.

WPFchangedns.ComboItems: Default DHCP Google Cloudflare Cloudflare_Malware Cloudflare_Malware_Adult Open_DNS Quad9 AdGuard_Ads_Trackers AdGuard_Ads_Trackers_Malware_Adult

WPFTweaksDisableExplorerAutoDiscovery.Content: File Explorer Automatic Folder Discovery - Disable
WPFTweaksDisableExplorerAutoDiscovery.Description: Windows Explorer automatically tries to guess the type of the folder based on its contents, slowing down the browsing experience. WARNING! Will disable File Explorer grouping.
InvokeScript:
# Previously detected folders
      $bags = "HKCU:\Software\Classes\Local Settings\Software\Microsoft\Windows\Shell\Bags"

      # Folder types lookup table
      $bagMRU = "HKCU:\Software\Classes\Local Settings\Software\Microsoft\Windows\Shell\BagMRU"

      # Flush Explorer view database
      Remove-Item -Path $bags -Recurse -Force
      Write-Host "Removed $bags"

      Remove-Item -Path $bagMRU -Recurse -Force
      Write-Host "Removed $bagMRU"

      # Every folder
      $allFolders = "HKCU:\Software\Classes\Local Settings\Software\Microsoft\Windows\Shell\Bags\AllFolders\Shell"

      if (!(Test-Path $allFolders)) {
        New-Item -Path $allFolders -Force
        Write-Host "Created $allFolders"
      }

      # Generic view
      New-ItemProperty -Path $allFolders -Name "FolderType" -Value "NotSpecified" -PropertyType String -Force
      Write-Host "Set FolderType to NotSpecified"

      Write-Host Please sign out and back in, or restart your computer to apply the changes!
UndoScript:
# Previously detected folders
      $bags = "HKCU:\Software\Classes\Local Settings\Software\Microsoft\Windows\Shell\Bags"

      # Folder types lookup table
      $bagMRU = "HKCU:\Software\Classes\Local Settings\Software\Microsoft\Windows\Shell\BagMRU"

      # Flush Explorer view database
      Remove-Item -Path $bags -Recurse -Force
      Write-Host "Removed $bags"

      Remove-Item -Path $bagMRU -Recurse -Force
      Write-Host "Removed $bagMRU"

      Write-Host Please sign out and back in, or restart your computer to apply the changes!

WPFTweaksDisableVBS.Description: Disables Virtualization-Based Security and Memory Integrity (HVCI) to improve gaming performance by 5-25%. Requires reboot. CAUTION: Reduces system security.
InvokeScript:
Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\DeviceGuard' -Name 'EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity' -Type DWord -Value 0 -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\DeviceGuard\Scenarios\HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity' -Name 'Enabled' -Type DWord -Value 0 -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa' -Name 'LsaCfgFlags' -Type DWord -Value 0 -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName 'VirtualMachinePlatform' -NoRestart -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName 'HypervisorPlatform' -NoRestart -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      bcdedit /set hypervisorlaunchtype off
      Write-Host 'VBS and Memory Integrity disabled. Reboot required.'
UndoScript:
Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\DeviceGuard' -Name 'EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity' -Type DWord -Value 1 -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\DeviceGuard\Scenarios\HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity' -Name 'Enabled' -Type DWord -Value 1 -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName 'VirtualMachinePlatform' -NoRestart -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      bcdedit /set hypervisorlaunchtype on
      Write-Host 'VBS and Memory Integrity enabled. Reboot required.'

WPFTweaksESPFix.Description: Resets ESP padding to fix May 2026 update error 0x800f0922 when KB5089549 fails at 35% during reboot. Run if update fails to install.

WPFToggleExplorerLaunchTo.Content: File Explorer - Opens to This PC
WPFToggleExplorerLaunchTo.Description: Changes File Explorer's default landing page from Quick Access to This PC, showing all drives and system folders immediately.

WPFToggleClassicContextMenu.Description: Restores the classic full right-click context menu in Windows 11 instead of the modern compact one. Requires restarting Explorer.
InvokeScript:
reg.exe add "HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID\{86ca1aa0-34aa-4e8b-a509-50c905bae2a2}\InprocServer32" /f /ve
      Invoke-WinUtilExplorerUpdate -action "restart"
      Write-Host "Classic context menu enabled — Explorer restarted."
UndoScript:
reg.exe delete "HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID\{86ca1aa0-34aa-4e8b-a509-50c905bae2a2}" /f
      Invoke-WinUtilExplorerUpdate -action "restart"
      Write-Host "Modern context menu restored — Explorer restarted."

WPFTweaksIconCache.Description: Deletes and rebuilds the Windows icon cache. Fixes corrupted, blank, or distorted desktop and File Explorer icons.
InvokeScript:
Get-Process explorer -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Stop-Process -Force
      Remove-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\IconCache.db" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Remove-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\iconcache*" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
      Start-Process explorer.exe
      Write-Host "Icon cache cleared and rebuilt."
UndoScript: Write-Host "Icon cache cannot be undone — it will rebuild automatically."

WPFToggleHideRecommended.Description: Hides the Recommended section entirely from the Windows 11 Start menu. Works on most Windows 11 builds via policy registry keys.

WPFToggleWebSearch.Description: Disables web search suggestions and Bing results from appearing in Windows Search and Start menu search. Local file and app search still works.

WPFToggleStoreAppLookup.Description: Disables the 'Look for an app in the Store' prompt when opening an unknown file type. Windows will only show locally installed apps.

WPFToggleNotificationCenter.Description: Disables the Notification Center (Win+N) and calendar flyout in Windows 11. The clock area will no longer open notifications.

WPFToggleAutoReboot.Description: Prevents Windows from automatically restarting the PC after installing updates. You will be prompted to schedule the restart instead.

WPFToggleLockScreen.Description: Disables the Windows lock screen entirely. The sign-in screen will appear directly when waking or locking the PC.

WPFToggleLastUserName.Description: Hides the last signed-in username from the Windows login screen. Adds a layer of privacy and security.

WPFTogglePasswordReveal.Description: Removes the eye-icon button next to password fields that reveals typed passwords.

WPFToggleCortana.Description: Completely disables Cortana via group policy. Does not affect search functionality.

WPFToggleDownloadsBlocking.Description: Prevents Windows from displaying security warnings when opening downloaded files from the internet.

WPFToggleClassicUAC.Description: Switches UAC to the classic (Windows 7-style) dimmed credential dialog instead of the modern Secure Desktop prompt.

WPFToggleMenuDelay.Description: Speeds up menu responsiveness by reducing the delay before submenus and context menus appear. Set to 0 for instant display.

WPFToggleDesktopAppStartup.Description: Removes the built-in startup delay for desktop applications, making them launch faster after login.

WPFToggleFastStartup.Description: Disables Windows Fast Startup (hybrid shutdown). Ensures a full shutdown and boot cycle, which can resolve driver and update issues.

WPFToggleAeroShake.Description: Disables Aero Shake, which minimizes all other windows when you shake the active window. Useful for gamers and users who trigger it accidentally.

WPFToggleAeroSnap.Description: Disables Aero Snap (window snapping/dragging to edges). Useful if you find the snapping behavior intrusive.

WPFToggleWindowsInk.Description: Disables the Windows Ink Workspace (pen/ink features). Useful if you don't use a stylus.

WPFToggleClockSeconds.Description: Shows seconds in the taskbar clock. The clock will display the current second alongside hours and minutes.

WPFToggleWindowsVersion.Description: Displays the Windows version and build number in the bottom-right corner of the desktop. Useful for beta testers and enthusiasts.

WPFToggleClassicVolume.Description: Restores the classic vertical volume control (Windows 7/8 style) instead of the modern horizontal slider in Windows 10/11.

WPFToggleCopilot.Description: Disables Microsoft Copilot AI assistant via policy.

WPFToggleWidgetsIcon.Description: Show or hide the Widgets icon (weather/news) on the taskbar.

WPFToggleChatIcon.Description: Show or hide the Chat/Teams icon on the taskbar.

WPFToggleSnapAssist.Description: Disables the Snap Assist flyout that appears when snapping windows.

WPFToggleSearchHighlights.Description: Disables search highlights and celebratory icons in the Windows Search flyout.

WPFToggleRoundedCorners.Description: Disables window rounded corners on Windows 11, reverting to sharp rectangular corners.

WPFToggleFindMyDevice.Description: Disables Find My Device location tracking for this device.

WPFToggleP2PUpdate.Description: Disables peer-to-peer delivery optimization for Windows updates, preventing your PC from uploading updates to other devices.

WPFToggleNavPaneAllFolders.Content: Explorer Nav Pane - Show All Folders
WPFToggleNavPaneAllFolders.Description: Shows all folders (Desktop, Downloads, Documents, etc.) directly in the File Explorer navigation pane.

WPFToggleCompactMode.Description: Enables compact mode in File Explorer, reducing padding between items for a denser layout.

WPFToggleAutoMaintenance.Description: Disables Windows automatic daily maintenance tasks. Can save system resources but may delay disk optimization and other scheduled maintenance.

WPFToggleUSBWriteProtect.Description: Prevents writing to USB storage devices. Useful for security-conscious environments to prevent data exfiltration via USB drives.

WPFToggleNewAppNotify.Description: Disables the 'New app installed' notifications that appear after installing new applications.

WPFToggleLastLogonInfo.Description: Displays information about the last successful logon on the sign-in screen, including username and time.

WPFToggleNetworkLockIcon.Content: Network Icon on Lock Screen - Hide
WPFToggleNetworkLockIcon.Description: Hides the network selection icon from the lock screen, preventing users from changing network connections before signing in.

WPFToggleLastActiveClick.Description: When enabled, clicking on a taskbar icon of a running app will switch to the last active window instead of cycling through all windows.

WPFToggleTaskbarFlash.Description: Stops taskbar buttons from flashing to alert you of background activity. Useful to avoid visual distraction.

WPFToggleWallpaperQuality.Description: Sets wallpaper JPEG import quality to maximum (100) for sharper desktop backgrounds. Default is 85.

WPFToggleVDesktopHover.Description: When enabled, hovering over a taskbar window of a different virtual desktop will automatically switch to that desktop.

WPFToggleBootAnimation.Description: Disables the Windows boot animation for slightly faster boot times. A simple loading indicator will be shown instead.

WPFToggleStoreAutoUpdate.Description: Prevents Microsoft Store from automatically downloading and installing app updates. Apps must be updated manually.

WPFToggleAdminShares.Description: Disables automatic creation of administrative shares (C$, ADMIN$, IPC$). Improves security by preventing hidden network access to drives.

WPFTweaksPhotoViewer.Description: Enables the classic Windows Photo Viewer and registers it to handle common image file types (.jpg, .png, .gif, .bmp, .tiff, etc.).
InvokeScript:
$regPath = 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Photo Viewer\Capabilities\FileAssociations'
      $assocs = @{
        '.bmp'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.dib'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.jfif'  = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.jpe'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.jpeg'  = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.jpg'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.jxr'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.png'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.gif'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.tiff'  = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.tif'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
        '.wdp'   = 'PhotoViewer.FileAssoc.Tiff'
      }
      if (-not (Test-Path $regPath)) { New-Item -Path $regPath -Force | Out-Null }
      foreach ($ext in $assocs.Keys) {
        Set-ItemProperty -Path $regPath -Name $ext -Value $assocs[$ext]
      }
      Write-Host "Windows Photo Viewer enabled for all common image types."
